blocks.content: Thursday. End of practice week two. Jonathan got picked up early — Mr. Tanaka, black car, engine already running. I walked out the front on my own.
Crystal was on the front steps.
Not standing. Sitting — legs crossed, a book open on her knee, headphones around her neck. She looked like she'd been there a while. Like the steps were hers.
She looked up before I said anything. She always did that. Like she heard me before I made a sound.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Good. You waiting for the bus?

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Yeah. The 34 runs at 5:15. I've got twenty minutes.
I sat down. Not because I'd decided to — just because the steps were there and she was there and stopping felt easier than walking past.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: You don't have to wait with me.
She smiled at that. Not wide. The same one from the locker hall — just enough.
